title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Alison Dumbie sends a letter expressing hope that the recipient will soon be reunited with Charles or Giles, noting that she will explain her own situation in future correspondence.

In a separate setting, Miss Marple discusses a letter with Jules and Gwendolyn. During this discussion, Jeeves reveals that his new home, Mount Edgecombe, was the site of a first-degree murder in the foyer approximately eighteen to twenty years ago. The victim was a beautiful woman known only by her Christian name. While Jeeves expresses a desire to investigate the mystery, Miss Marple becomes visibly distressed. She warns the newlyweds to stay away from the matter, arguing that because the crime was never reported or suspected by the community, investigating the past could disrupt their peaceful lives and cause them pain. Despite her warnings, Jeeves remains determined to investigate the murder that occurred in his own house.

Upon returning to St. Mary Mead, Miss Marple appears exhausted and absent-minded, causing concern for her neighbors and her maid, Evelyn. She summons her long-time friend and physician, Dr. Hedock. Although the doctor attributes her weakness to the draining atmosphere of London, Miss Marple uses the pretext of needing restorative sea air to request a prescription for travel. She reveals her intention to go to the town of Dartmouth. When Dr. Hedock questions her motive, Miss Marple responds by posing a hypothetical scenario regarding the discovery of an old, unreported murder.
